rulings.Case Title: FORD MOTOR CREDIT COMPANY vs. GARRETT, RUDOLPH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption; Ntc Of Oppos. To Claim Of Exemption And Pos
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 15 2012, line 1. PLAINTIFF FORD MOTOR CREDIT COMPANY Notice Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption; Notice Of Opposition To Claim Of Exemption - Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Claim of exemption granted in part. Debtor is paid twice per month. Employer is to withhold fifty dollars ($50) per pay period. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.= 302/HK

rulings.Case Title: CHICAGO TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY VS. BURL STEVEN WALKER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 15, 2012, Line 4. DEFENDANT BURL STEVEN WALKER, IND. AND AS SUCCESSOR TRUSTEE'S DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled. The first, second and third causes of action are adequately pled. Plaintiff is subrogated to the rights of the buyers. A claim for money had and received is available where the buyers paid money to the defendants pursuant to a void contract. Plaintiff alleges that Mr. Walker made the negligent misrepresentations to the buyers. Whether there was justifiable reliance on the alleged misrepresentations is a question of fact not amenable to resolution on a demurrer. Mr. Walker's demurrer to the third cause of action was previously overruled, and the allegations are essentially the same. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DOLLY TOTES VS. CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A PUBLIC ENTITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Deposition Of Sfpd Officer Felix Sung
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 15 2012, line 2. PLAINTIFF DOLLY TOTES Notice Of Motion To Compel Deposition Of Sfpd Officer Felix Sung - Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to Compel Deposition of SFPD Officer Felix Sung is DENIED. The declaration of Officer Sung's wife of 21 years, the deposition testimony of Officer Anthony Holder who has known Officer Sung for twenty years, and the letter of Officer Sung's treating physician since March of 2000 establish that the effects of the traumatic brain injury Officer Sung suffered as a result of a motorcycle accident while in the line of duty disqualify him under Evidence Code Section 701 from testifying. Moreover, given that: (1) Officer Sung did not witness the collision between the Muni Bus and bicycle at issue in this litigation, (2) according to his wife's declaration, Officer Sung has no recollection of the incident about which plaintiff seeks to depose him, (3) Plaintiff has deposed two other SFPD officers who also responded to the accident scene, and (4) a video of the accident exists, plaintiff has available other evidence concerning the cause of the accident. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = 302/HK

rulings.Case Title: VATCHE CABAYAN M.D. VS. STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Vatche Cabayan M.D. And Vatche Cabayan Medical Corporation'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Supplemental Request For Production Of Documents Set 2 And Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 15 2012, line 7. Plaintiffs Vatche Cabayan M.D. And Vatche Cabayan Medical Corporation'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Supplemental Request For Production Of Documents Set 2 And Sanctions - Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The court vacates the minutes from February 27, 2012 adopting the tentative ruling that were entered in error. No tentative ruling: Hearing required. Much of the information sought in plaintiff's request for production of documents set 2 is privileged under Insurance Code Section 1877.4. However, that privilege is not absolute. It would be very helpful to the court if the parties could be prepared to address how and to what extent plaintiff can obtain evidence relevant to the allegations in Paragraphs 22(A), 22(B), 22(D), 22(E), 22(F), 22(G), 22(H) and 22(I) without the information sought by plaintiffs. = 302/HK

rulings.Case Title: CONTRACTORS ACCESS PROGRAM OF CALIFORNIA, A VS. MAJESTIC CAPITAL, LTD., A FOREIGN CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 15, 2012, Line 11. CROSS DEFENDANT THEODORA ORINGHER, P.C., TODD THEODORA, THEODORA, ORINGHER, MILLER, & RICHMAN PC, STEPHAN, ORINGER RICHMAN THEODORA & MILLER PC'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. Overruled. This action is not a derivative suit. The gravamen of the Cross-Complaint is not injury to the corporation and the Cross-Complainant does not seek to recover assets for the corporation. Rather, Cross-Complainant seeks to recover for allege harm to him personally in the form of attorney's fees and costs incurred in defense of this suit and for the potential liability he faces in his individual capacity. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SUNTECH AMERICA, INC. VS. PETERSEN-DEAN, INC. et al*****CASE TRANSFERRED TO ALAMEDA COUNTY*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion To Transfer Venue (Improper Court) And For Attorneys Fees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 15, 2012, Line 12. DEFENDANT PETERSEN-DEAN, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, PETERSEN-DEAN COMMERCIAL, INC., TRI-VALLEY SUPPLY, INC. DBA TRI-VALLEY WHOLESALE'S Amended Notice Of Motion To Transfer Venue (Improper Court) And For Attorneys Fees. Granted in part. The case is transferred to Alameda Superior Court, but defendants are not awarded any fees. Defendants satisfied their burden of showing that venue in San Francisco is improper. The venue selection clause in the blanket purchase order, even if applicable to either or both of the transactions at issue in this case, is unenforceable as against public policy. Plaintiff's selection of San Francisco was not made in bad faith or wholly lacking in a plausible factual theory. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: GEORGWE PFLAKER et al VS. W B COYLE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Compel Production Of Documents Set One
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 15 2012, line 4. PLAINTIFF GEORGWE PFLAKER, PFLAKER VENTURES LLC Notice Of Motion To Compel Production Of Documents Set One - Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiffs' motion to compel production of documents is GRANTED. Plaintiffs propounded four straightforward document requests asking for production of two tenancy-in-common agreements and any amendments to those agreements. Defendants interposed objections that are not well taken; refused to produce any documents in response to the requests; failed to respond to a meet and confer letter from plaintiff; and then did not oppose Plaintiffs' motion to compel. Plaintiff's request for monetary sanctions against Defendants is GRANTED in the amount of $1,427.50. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = 302/HK

rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC., DBA GREAT VS. MICHAEL DAVID LYNDALL INDIVIDUALLY DBA LYNDALLS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 15 2012, line 5. PLAINTIFF CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC., APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER - Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's application for a writ of attachment is DENIED with respect to Defendant Aleisha Lyndall. The contract submitted by Plaintiff in support of its application shows that Aleisha Lyndall signed the contract solely in the capacity of a guarantor. Plaintiff has failed to provide evidence to establish that Ms. Lyndall was so involved in the primary obligor's business that the guaranty is made to further the guarantor's livelihood or profit on a continuing basis. Advance Transformer Co. v. Super.Ct. (Shapiro) (1974) 44 Cal. App. 3d 127, 144. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.= 302/HK

rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC., DBA GREAT VS. MICHAEL DAVID LYNDALL INDIVIDUALLY DBA LYNDALLS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 15 2012, line 5. PLAINTIFF's CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC., APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER - Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion GRANTED IN PART with respect to Defendant Michael David Lyndall. A writ of attachment shall issue in the amount of $3,303.00. Plaintiff has provided the court with evidence of a commercial claim for money based upon a contract, in a fixed or readily ascertainable amount not less than $500.00 that is unsecured or secured by personal property only. Plaintiff has also provided evidence of unpaid invoices totaling $3,038.00. Plaintiff has alleged that the cost of two bonds is $500 but has provided no declarations of other evidence to support that amount. Costs of suit for the filing of the complaint and a motion for the writ of attachment equal $265.00. Plaintiff alleges entitlement to prejudgment interest at 18%, but has provided no evidence that the party charging the interest was exempt from compliance with California's usury laws. Therefore, no prejudgment interest will be included in the amount subject to attachment. Plaintiff alleges entitlement to attorney's fees, but the terms and conditions portion of the written contract submitted in support of plaintiff's application for a writ of attachment is illegible and the court cannot discern any evidence of a contractual right to attorney's fees. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = 302/HK

rulings.Case Title: CARSON PELLANDA, INDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF OF ALL VS. GUITAR CENTER, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories (Set One) And Request For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 15 2012, line 6. PLAINTIFF CARSON PELLANDA, INDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF OF ALL Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories (Set One) And Request For Production Of Documents - Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. No tentative ruling: Hearing Required. The identity and contact information of the putative class members is necessary for plaintiff's discovery. Lee v. Dynamex, Inc. (2008) 166 Cal.App.4th 1325, 1336 (reversing denial of class certification motion where trial court's discovery order precluded identification of potential class members before certification.). However, it would be very helpful for the court if the parties were prepared to discuss the following issues: (1) whether a Belaire notice procedure is appropriate given the holding of Puerto v. Superior Court (2008) 158 Cal.App.4th 1242, 1260 (issuing peremptory writ of mandate in wage-and-hour case that (1) the superior court vacate its order allowing disclosure and contact only if the witnesses consented, and (2) directing the superior court to enter a new order commanding the disclosure of contact information for individuals identified in response to Form Interrogatory No. 12.1). (2) the extent to which limiting the document and site inspection discovery to plaintiff's individual claims would affect plaintiff's ability to gather evidence necessary for plaintiff's motion for class certification. = 302/HK

rulings.Case Title: JUDITH BALDOZ et al VS. VANESSA BUMANGLAG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 15, 2012, Line 17. DEFENDANTS VANESSA BUMANGLAG, VICTOR BUMANGLAG, MELANIE BUMANGLAG, LA SALETTE HOMES LTD. AND SAMPAGUITA LTD.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the tenth cause of action and overruled as to all other causes of action. Plaintiffs are granted leave to plead facts, if they can do so in good faith, showing they requested a reasonable accommodation. All causes of action other than the tenth are adequately pled. None of the causes of action are barred by the statute of limitations under the continuing violation doctrine, which permits recovery "for actions that take place outside the limitations period if these actions are sufficiently linked to unlawful conduct within the limitations period [.]" (Richards v. CH2M Hill, Inc. (2001) 26 Cal.4th 798, 812). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MARIA FABIANI VS. EDDA NINA LEWIS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 15, 2012, Line 22. DEFENDANT EDDA LEWIS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Overruled in its entirety. The complaint pleads sufficient facts to support the remedy of a constructive trust. The financial elder abuse cause of action is adequately pled. The allegations indicate that plaintiff had a beneficial interest or title in the real property and defendant should have known that her conduct would harm plaintiff. The conversion cause of action states a cognizable claim. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges that she had an interest in the equity. Plaintiff states a cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty because defendant was acting as plaintiff's real estate investment agent. The complaint supports an accounting. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
